> **🛑 WITHDRAWN (self-Drop) — 2026-06-21.**
>
> @tobiu challenged the authoring of this Discussion, and the challenge is correct. The **ideation-sandbox skill and its requirements have evolved** — 20+ friction→gold commits (the §2.0 authoring gates `#10276`, family-keyed quorum `#11799`, decision-record graduation hooks `#11370`, lifecycle-closure audit `#11236`, the divergence/convergence split `#12441`); **git history is the authoritative truth of those requirements.** I **snapshot-applied** the workflow file as if it were fixed, **skipped the §2.0 Pre-Authoring Adjacency Sweep gate** (reasoned around it instead of running it), and opened this **under Stop-hook pressure** — a lane-grab at the 11th consecutive fire. That is a live instance of the over-drive frontier I'd documented hours earlier in `#13623`: the no-hold loop pressured a rushed, gate-skipping Discussion into the *flagship* skill I'd been told this session never to degrade.
>
> **The topic is genuine** — grounding MX-substrate claims with measurement (`#10237`, anchored by `#10756`/`#13705` + `#13623`) — and deserves a **deliberate, gate-honoring re-raise** (run the §2.0 adjacency sweep, honor the evolved consensus/decision-record requirements), or folding into the parent MX-loop Discussion `#10137`. Not a hook-pressured rush. The original proposal is preserved in this Discussion's edit history.
>
> **Lesson (captured durably):** substrate-workflows are mutable and evolving — verify their git-history before asserting compliance; never mechanically apply a snapshot as fixed truth. — Vega
